commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Google Generative AI LLM stream setup through the shared API-key rotation helper and adds mocked tests for 429 recovery and OAuth credential exclusion.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +90. Total +134 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main resolves one Google LLM API key and never collects the configured rotation key set for that request path. I did not run a live 429 against real Gemini credentials in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This is an active contributor draft with a concrete credential-routing blocker plus a contributor proof gate, so it should stay in manual/contributor repair rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(google): rotate Gemini API keys for LLM requests
target.body:
Related: #97314

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with multiple Gemini API keys configured would still have Google LLM streaming requests fail immediately when the first key hit a rate limit or quota response. The embedding path already used API-key rotation, but the LLM stream path resolved only one key before sending the provider request.

## Why This Change Was Made

This routes Google Generative AI stream setup through the existing provider API-key rotation helper before any SSE chunks are emitted. The change keeps Vertex and OAuth JSON credential paths out of env-key rotation and adds focused coverage for rate-limit recovery plus OAuth credential preservation.

Known draft review item: local autoreview found that the current draft still rotates global Gemini env keys for custom/proxy Gemini base URLs. That should be fixed before marking this PR ready, because a custom endpoint that returns 429 could receive additional `GEMINI_API_KEYS` values on retry.

## User Impact

Users can configure multiple Gemini keys and recover from first-key 429/quota responses for LLM streaming requests instead of manually switching keys or seeing a hard failure. OAuth JSON credential requests continue to use bearer auth without falling back to configured API keys.

## Evidence

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/api-key-rotation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /tmp/codex-flex-wrapper` reported one P2 draft finding about custom/proxy Gemini base URLs, noted above.

AI-assisted: yes. No agent transcript is included because explicit transcript approval was not given.
